items.title: USDA posts SNAP food restriction waivers with 2026 start dates (Texas April 1; Florida April 20)
items.summary: USDA’s Food and Nutrition Service posted a list of approved SNAP food restriction waivers that restrict purchases of items like sweetened drinks and candy, with target implementation dates across 2026. The page lists Texas starting 2026-04-01 (sweetened drinks and candy), Florida starting 2026-04-20 (soda, energy drinks, candy, and prepared desserts), and Idaho starting 2026-02-15 (soda and candy), among other states.

items.title: CMS issues guidance for Oct. 1, 2026 limits on federal Medicaid/CHIP matching funds for certain noncitizens
items.summary: CMS issued guidance to states for a statutory change that limits when states can claim federal matching funds for full Medicaid and CHIP benefits for certain noncitizens beginning 2026-10-01. CMS notes federal match remains available for treatment of emergency medical conditions and that the change does not apply to the option to cover lawfully residing children and pregnant people in Medicaid/CHIP.

items.title: HUD: PHAs and multifamily owners must do interim reexaminations when households add/remove members (HOTMA guidance update)
items.summary: HUD Notice PIH 2026-09 / H 2026-05 (issued 2026-04-13) revises HOTMA implementation guidance to require interim reexaminations whenever a household adds or removes members (including foster adults/children and live-in aides), even if adjusted income does not change. HUD also provides a limited exception for agencies/owners with a written policy not to conduct interim reexams for income increases during the last 3 months of a recertification period.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

items.title: SSA: 2026 SSI federal benefit rates increased with the January 2026 COLA
items.summary: SSA says the 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment effective January 2026 increases the maximum federal SSI payment to $994/month for an eligible individual and $1,491/month for an eligible couple. SSA also lists $498/month as the maximum federal payment for an essential person.

items.title: USDA: FY2026 WIC fruit/vegetable cash-value benefit amounts set; states must authorize at least one non-fresh option by April 2026
items.summary: USDA WIC Policy Memorandum 2026-2 sets FY2026 monthly fruit/vegetable cash-value benefit amounts at $26 for children, $48 for pregnant/postpartum participants, and $52 for fully/mostly breastfeeding participants. The memo also notes WIC food package changes require state agencies to authorize at least one form of fruits/vegetables beyond fresh (frozen, canned, and/or dried) by April 2026.

items.title: Federal FAFSA deadline for 2025–26 is June 30, 2026 (with corrections due Sept. 12, 2026)
items.summary: Federal Student Aid lists the federal FAFSA submission deadline for the 2025–26 award year as 11:59 p.m. Central Time on 2026-06-30. The site also lists the deadline for corrections/updates as 11:59 p.m. Central Time on 2026-09-12.

items.title: IRS: EITC tables for tax year 2025 include max credit $8,046 (3+ kids) and investment income limit $11,950
items.summary: IRS EITC tables for tax year 2025 list maximum credit amounts of $649 (no qualifying children), $4,328 (1 child), $7,152 (2 children), and $8,046 (3+ children). IRS also lists an investment income limit of $11,950 or less for claiming EITC for tax year 2025.

items.title: HUD: HOTMA final rule compliance date for CPD programs extended to Jan. 1, 2026 (with option for earlier compliance)
items.summary: HUD’s CPD guidance says the HOTMA final rule compliance date for CPD programs that use HUD’s 24 CFR Part 5 income regulations was extended to 2026-01-01. HUD also notes grantees ready to comply may set an earlier compliance date between 2024-01-01 and 2026-01-01, and that certain income safe harbors may be implemented prior to the compliance date.
